content:
# Delta Upscale Benchmark — 1080p vs 1440p Proxy Comparison (2026-04-11)

Follow-up to finding `019d7a7b-458c-7033-ad34-56d84fdfb0a6` (first real run).
Branch: `feat/upscale-bench` @ `5d4b8f5` (repos/dorea)
Clip:   `footage/raw/2025-11-01/DJI_20251101111428_0055_D_3s.MP4`
Gold:   shared cache (gold is proxy-independent; symlinked for the 1440 run)

## Run config

Same clip, same 3 auto-selected frames `[0, 30, 180]`, same 8 methods
(`sr_realesrgan` soft-skipped), same gold tensors. Only difference:
`--proxy-size 1080` (default) vs `--proxy-size 1440`. This is a 2×
linear / 4× area vs 1.5× linear / 2.25× area upscale to 4K.

Outputs on disk:
- 1080: `working/upscale_bench/`
- 1440: `working/upscale_bench_1440/`

## Aggregate ΔE (lower is better)

| method | 1080 | 1440 | Δ |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **bilinear** | 4.988 | **4.574** | **−8.3%** ← new ΔE winner at 1440 |
| bicubic | 5.027 | 4.586 | −8.8% |
| lanczos3 | 5.025 | 4.585 | −8.8% |
| guided_filter | **4.926** | 4.655 | −5.5% ← was winner at 1080, now worst non-SR |
| joint_bilateral | 5.009 | 4.602 | −8.1% |
| asymmetric_bilateral | 5.012 | 4.605 | −8.1% |
| sr_maxine | 5.104 | 4.782 | −6.3% ← still strictly worst |
| higher_proxy | ~0 | ~0 | (IS the gold) |

## Aggregate SSIM (higher is better)

| method | 1080 | 1440 | Δ |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| bilinear | 0.9476 | 0.9589 | +1.2% |
| **bicubic** | 0.9457 | **0.9598** | +1.5% ← new SSIM winner at 1440 |
| lanczos3 | 0.9452 | 0.9597 | +1.5% |
| **guided_filter** | **0.9501** | 0.9538 | +0.4% ← was SSIM winner, now mid |
| joint_bilateral | 0.9461 | 0.9563 | +1.1% |
| asymmetric_bilateral | 0.9461 | 0.9563 | +1.1% |
| sr_maxine | 0.9185 | 0.9439 | +2.8% (biggest relative gain, still last) |

## Upscale-only timing (ms; FPS in parens)

| method | 1080 | 1440 | Δ |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| bilinear | 0.68 (1477) | 0.68 (1477) | 0% |
| bicubic | 8.88 (113) | 8.99 (111) | −1.6% |
| joint_bilateral | 14.73 (68) | 15.25 (66) | −3.5% |
| asymmetric_bilateral | 14.72 (68) | 15.26 (66) | −3.5% |
| lanczos3 | 14.98 (67) | 16.67 (60) | −10.3% |
| sr_maxine | 47.14 (21.2) | 60.59 (16.5) | −22.2% |
| guided_filter | 55.60 (18.0) | 55.40 (18.1) | +0.3% |
| higher_proxy | 4688.15 (0.21) | 3983.64 (0.25) | +17.7% |

## End-to-end timing (ms; FPS in parens) — upscale + apply_delta

Dominated by the ~28 ms fixed cost of `_apply_delta` (4K OKLab↔RGB round-trip).

| method | 1080 e2e ms (fps) | 1440 e2e ms (fps) |
|---|---:|---:|
| bilinear | 28.68 (34.9) | 28.69 (34.9) |
| bicubic | 36.90 (27.1) | 37.00 (27.0) |
| joint_bilateral | 42.72 (23.4) | 43.25 (23.1) |
| asymmetric_bilateral | 42.71 (23.4) | 43.25 (23.1) |
| lanczos3 | 42.93 (23.3) | 44.63 (22.4) |
| sr_maxine | 75.00 (13.3) | 88.19 (11.3) |
| guided_filter | 83.39 (12.0) | 83.12 (12.0) |
| higher_proxy | 4724.93 (0.21) | 4026.43 (0.25) |

## Findings

### 1. Quality winner flipped from guided_filter to bilinear

At 1080, edge-aware filtering bought ~1% ΔE over bilinear (guided_filter was
the best non-trivial method). At 1440, the ranking inverts: `bilinear` is the
best non-trivial method on ΔE, and `guided_filter` becomes the **worst**
non-trivial method (+1.8% ΔE vs bilinear).

**Interpretation:** Edge-aware methods earn their cost by recovering
high-frequency edges from a low-res signal. When the proxy is already close
to target (1.5× vs 2×), there is less edge detail to recover and the cost-to-
artifact ratio of the edge-aware step starts to look net negative — the
reconstruction introduces small artifacts that the tighter proxy doesn't
benefit enough to offset.

### 2. sr_maxine is strictly worst at BOTH resolutions — and the gap persists

Learned SR (Maxine VideoSuperRes) ranks dead last on every aggregate metric
in both runs. At 1440p, Maxine's ΔE (4.78) is still 4.6% worse than bilinear's
(4.57), and its SSIM (0.944) is still 1.6% below bilinear's (0.959).

This strengthens the 1080 finding: **learned SR is not a free lunch for the
delta pathway regardless of proxy resolution**. Maxine's hallucinated high-
frequency content is always noise relative to the smooth OKLab delta signal.
A better proxy input does NOT help it catch up — it just raises the floor
for everyone equally.

### 3. ~8% ΔE improvement going 1080 → 1440 for all classical methods

Every classical / edge-aware method gains ~5–9% on ΔE when fed a 1440p proxy
instead of 1080p. This is a real, measurable "info ceiling" improvement —
the tighter proxy preserves more delta information.

Bilinear: 4.988 → 4.574 (−8.3%)
SSIM also improves uniformly by +0.4% to +2.8%.

**Tradeoff the bench cannot decide:** 1440p proxy means RAUNE runs inference
on 2.25× more pixels upstream (not measured in this bench — `_apply_delta` is
the only thing timed past the upscale). Whether ~8% ΔE reduction is worth the
RAUNE compute bump is a production decision pending a separate end-to-end
measurement that includes RAUNE proxy inference.

### 4. Method-to-method spread narrows at 1440p

At 1080p, the spread across non-trivial methods was wider: best (guided_filter
4.926) to worst-non-SR (bilinear 4.988) ≈ 1.3%, plus sr_maxine at 5.104 ≈
3.6% total. At 1440p, the six classical/edge-aware methods collapse into a
1.8% band (4.574–4.655), and sr_maxine is the only outlier.

**Implication:** method selection matters less at higher proxy resolutions.
The choice between bilinear, bicubic, and the edge-aware methods becomes
effectively cosmetic above 1080p proxy input.

### 5. Upscale-only FPS is nearly proxy-size-insensitive for most methods

Only two methods show meaningful FPS regression at 1440p:
- **sr_maxine**: −22% (21.2 → 16.5 fps). Its SR network scales with input.
- **lanczos3**: −10% (67 → 60 fps). Separable 1-D taps scale with proxy width.

`bilinear`, `bicubic`, `guided_filter`, `joint_bilateral`, and
`asymmetric_bilateral` are essentially unchanged — their cost is dominated
by the 4K output side, not the proxy input side.

`higher_proxy` is ~18% faster at 1440p (likely because RAUNE-at-4K internal
scaling is better aligned with 1440 than 1080).

### 6. End-to-end FPS is dominated by the 28 ms apply_delta floor

Every non-SR method pays a fixed ~28.68 ms for the 4K OKLab↔RGB round-trip
in `_apply_delta`. Even bilinear's 0.68 ms upscale is swamped by this floor.
The apparent FPS spread at e2e (bilinear 35 fps vs guided_filter 12 fps) is
really the apply_delta floor plus method cost, not pure method differentiation.

**Real-time 4K playback thresholds (upscale + apply_delta, excluding RAUNE):**
- **Clears 30 fps**: only `bilinear` (35 fps at both resolutions)
- **Clears 24 fps**: `bilinear`, `bicubic` (27 fps), close to threshold `joint_bilateral` / `asymmetric_bilateral` / `lanczos3` (22–23 fps)
- **Below 24 fps**: `guided_filter` (12 fps), `sr_maxine` (11–13 fps), `higher_proxy` (0.2 fps)

## Production recommendation (unchanged, stronger)

Keep production on `bilinear`. It is on the pareto frontier at both proxy
resolutions — fastest AND best ΔE at 1440p, fastest AND within ~1% of best
ΔE at 1080p. There is no quality-justified reason to switch upscale methods.

**Open question the bench cannot answer**: should production run RAUNE on a
1440p proxy instead of 1080p? You get ~8% ΔE reduction for ~2.25× RAUNE
proxy-inference compute. A separate end-to-end benchmark that includes the
RAUNE inference stage would be needed to decide. Candidate follow-up.

## Caveats

- `wall_time_ms_end_to_end` in this bench only covers upscale + apply_delta.
  It does NOT include frame decode, RAUNE proxy inference, LUT application,
  or any I/O. Do not read these FPS numbers as pipeline throughput.
- Only 3 frames (0, 30, 180) were measured. Frame 180 dominates the aggregate
  (~80% of total ΔE) because it's the hardest frame (dive scene, bubbles,
  two divers, surface light). A larger sample would tighten the ranking.
- `sr_realesrgan` was not installed in either run (soft-skipped).
- The 1440 run reused the 1080 run's gold cache via symlink — safe because
  gold is RAUNE-at-4K of the raw frame and has no dependency on proxy size.
